questions.question: Briefly explain the concept of 'Location Transparency' in a Distributed File System.
expectedPoints: Definition: The user does not need to know the physical location of a file to access it.
Mechanism: Files are accessed using a global, logical namespace rather than physical server addresses.
Benefit: Simplifies user interaction and allows files to be moved without disrupting clients.

questions.question: Describe the three main abstract components of a file service architecture and explain the interaction between them when a client opens a file named '/projects/report.txt'.
expectedPoints: Component 1: Client Module - Intercepts the local 'open' call from the application.
Component 2: Directory Service - The Client Module contacts the Directory Service to resolve the path '/projects/report.txt' into a Unique File Identifier (UFID).
Component 3: Flat File Service - The Client Module uses the obtained UFID to contact the Flat File Service to begin reading the file's content (e.g., metadata, data blocks).
Interaction Flow: The request flows from Client -> Directory Service (for lookup) -> Flat File Service (for data). The response (file data) flows back along the same path.
Role of Caching: The Client Module may cache the UFID and file data for future requests to improve performance.
